title: FileInputFormatCounter and InvalidInputException in Hadoop MapReduce
description:
The 'lib/input' directory in Hadoop MapReduce architecture contains crucial components for input handling and error management. The 'FileInputFormatCounter.java' file defines an enumeration, `FileInputFormatCounter`, used by Task classes to track bytes read during the input format phase of a MapReduce job. This enumeration aids in monitoring job performance.

On the other hand, 'InvalidInputException.java' encapsulates a list of input-related exceptions (`IOException`) into a single `InvalidInputException`. This class simplifies error handling for users by providing a comprehensive list of input issues instead of dealing with each one individually. It includes methods to retrieve the list of problems and a concatenated message summarizing all the issues.

Both components, through their specific roles, contribute to the robustness and efficiency of input processing in Hadoop MapReduce, adhering to the Observer and Composite patterns for monitoring and managing input-related events and exceptions respectively.
